layout_description: A two-column layout with a fixed-width left sidebar and a flexible main content area that fills the remaining width. The sidebar is persistent — it remains visible during navigation and does not scroll with the content. The main area is a general-purpose region that can host any primary view: a list, a form, a data table, a feed, or a detail panel. An optional third panel can slide in from the right edge to display contextual detail without replacing the main view. The sidebar typically contains a wordmark or app logo at the top, followed by a vertical stack of navigation links grouped by section, and a user profile or account control pinned to the bottom.
html_structure:
import { SidebarProvider, Sidebar, SidebarHeader, SidebarContent, SidebarGroup, SidebarGroupLabel, SidebarGroupContent, SidebarMenu, SidebarMenuItem, SidebarMenuButton, SidebarFooter, SidebarTrigger } from '@/components/ui/sidebar' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Toaster } from '@/components/ui/sonner' export default function App() { return (
{/* Sidebar */} {/* Wordmark or app logo */} {/* Avatar + user name + account menu */} U {/* Main content area */}
{/* Page title / breadcrumb */}
{/* Primary view: rendered page content */}
{/* Optional: Sheet-based detail panel */} {/* ... */}
) }

sidebar.block_alternative: app-sidebar — use this block if the sidebar needs team/workspace switching, nested nav, or a search field built in
typical_content: App logo or wordmark in SidebarHeader
Navigation links as SidebarMenuButton inside SidebarMenuItem
Links grouped by section using SidebarGroup + SidebarGroupLabel
User avatar and account DropdownMenu in SidebarFooter

components.page-header: Plain flex header row with SidebarTrigger + heading
components.content-body: Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardContent — primary grouping unit
components.data-display: data-table block (via @/components/blocks/data-table) or Table + TableHeader + TableBody + TableRow + TableCell

typical_content: Page heading as h1 or CardTitle
Primary action Button (one default variant per view)
Content pattern appropriate to the page: table, form, feed, or detail record

detail-panel.role: Contextual overlay for a selected item — optional, does not replace content-area

components.panel: Sheet + SheetContent (side='right') + SheetHeader + SheetTitle + SheetDescription + SheetFooter

typical_content: Selected record detail in Card or plain layout
Edit form using Input, Textarea, Select, Label

constraints: Always wrap the entire layout in SidebarProvider — Sidebar and SidebarTrigger depend on its context
SidebarTrigger must appear in the main content header so the user can collapse/expand the sidebar
The active SidebarMenuButton must reflect the current route — use a conditional className or data-active prop
The content-area is a single scroll region — do not nest a second overflow-y-auto container inside it without explicit need
On mobile viewports the Sidebar component handles collapse automatically — do not add custom mobile logic
The detail panel (Sheet) is additive — opening it must not remove or obscure the content-area
One default variant Button per visible area — all other actions use outline, ghost, or secondary variants
agent_instructions: Scaffold the SidebarProvider + Sidebar shell first, then infer 4–6 navigation items from the app description and populate SidebarMenu with them, grouped into 1–2 SidebarGroups. Set the first nav item as active. Populate the content-area with the most likely primary view for the app — if it manages data, use a Table or data-table block; if it is a productivity tool, use a Card-based layout. Place a user avatar with initials in SidebarFooter. Do not leave any zone empty — use realistic placeholder content. If the app description implies a detail view (clicking a row opens more info), implement it as a Sheet sliding in from the right.

layout_description: A high-density overview page intended to surface the most important information about a system at a glance. The page does not support deep navigation within itself — it is a read-mostly summary, not an editing surface. The layout is divided into horizontal bands: a page header with title and time-range or filter controls, a row of metric cards showing key numbers, one or more chart regions showing trends or distributions, and optionally a compact table or activity feed at the bottom. Everything is visible without drilling down — the dashboard rewards scanning, not reading.
html_structure:
import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { ChartContainer, ChartTooltip, ChartTooltipContent } from '@/components/ui/chart' import { AreaChart, Area, BarChart, Bar, XAxis, YAxis, CartesianGrid, ResponsiveContainer } from 'recharts' export default function App() { return (
{/* Page header */}

{/* Time range or filter Select */}
{/* Stat cards */}
{/* Each stat: Card > CardHeader (label) + CardContent (value + delta Badge) */}
{/* Charts */}
{/* AreaChart or BarChart via recharts */}
{/* Bottom table or activity feed (optional) */} {/* Compact table or list of recent items */}
) }

stat-cards.role: Single key numbers — the most important metrics

components.label: CardTitle with text-sm font-medium text-muted-foreground
components.delta: Badge (default variant for positive, secondary for neutral, destructive for negative) or plain p with text-green-600 / text-red-600

stat-cards.block_alternative: section-cards block (from @/components/blocks/section-cards) — use when 4+ cards with consistent delta formatting are needed
typical_content: 3–5 cards: one per key metric relevant to the app domain
Each card: muted label + large value + optional delta vs previous period
do_not_place: More than 5 cards in a row

components.wrapper: ChartContainer (from @/components/ui/chart) — required, handles sizing and config
components.chart-types: AreaChart, BarChart, LineChart, PieChart from recharts (recharts must be added as a dependency)

chart-area.block_alternative: chart-area-interactive block (from @/components/blocks/chart-area-interactive) — use for a pre-built interactive area chart with date range toggle
typical_content: Line or area chart of a primary metric over time
Bar chart comparing categories or time periods

table-preview.role: Compact tabular summary — top-N or most recent items

components.simple: Table + TableHeader + TableBody + TableRow + TableHead + TableCell (from @/components/ui/table)
components.advanced: data-table block (from @/components/blocks/data-table) — use when sorting, filtering, or pagination is needed

typical_content: Link to full table view rather than paginating inline
do_not_place: Full paginated tables — link out to a dedicated table view instead

constraints: All data shown must be synchronously available on mount — no lazy-loaded drill-downs embedded in the dashboard itself
Stat cards must always show a label — never a raw number with no context
Charts must always be wrapped in ChartContainer — never use recharts components directly without it
Every zone needs an empty state using the Empty component if data is absent
Do not place primary create/edit actions on the dashboard — link out to dedicated views
Use hardcoded realistic sample data — not lorem ipsum numbers like 1234 or 9999
Avoid nesting more than two chart columns on narrow viewports — stack to a single column below 768px
agent_instructions: Identify the app domain from the description and infer 3–5 relevant metrics for the stat cards (e.g. for a finance app: Balance, Income, Expenses, Transactions this month). Generate realistic sample data arrays for charts — at least 7 data points for time series. Implement two charts: prefer an AreaChart for a time-series trend and a BarChart for a categorical comparison. Wrap both in ChartContainer with a config object. Populate the bottom table with 5–8 realistic rows relevant to the domain. Include a date-range Select in the header. Add recharts to dependencies.

layout_description: A focused, single-task flow that guides the user through a sequence of steps to complete a goal. Each step occupies the full viewport and presents exactly one decision or input group — the user cannot see or interact with other steps until they complete the current one. A progress indicator at the top communicates position and total length. Navigation is linear: a primary CTA advances to the next step, a back control returns to the previous. The final step shows a confirmation summary before a submit action. The overall chrome is minimal — no sidebar, no persistent navigation.

progress-bar.role: Orientation — tells the user where they are and how much remains

components.step-counter: Plain text: 'Step N of M' using text-sm text-muted-foreground
components.step-label: Plain text showing current step title using text-sm font-medium

do_not_place: Any interactive element other than an optional 'Save and exit' escape hatch

typical_content: Short imperative step title: 'Name your project', 'Choose a plan', 'Review your order'
One sentence of guidance in CardDescription

step-body.role: The single task for this step — rendered conditionally based on step index

components.field-group: Label + Input (or Textarea, Select, RadioGroup, Checkbox) — each field wrapped in a div with flex flex-col gap-1.5
components.validated-fields: Form + FormField + FormItem + FormLabel + FormControl + FormMessage (when using react-hook-form + zod)
components.choice-grid: RadioGroup + RadioGroupItem for mutually exclusive options
components.review-summary: Separator between sections, plain dl/dd or Key–Value pairs using text-sm on the final step

typical_content: 2–5 tightly related form fields for one logical decision
A RadioGroup for single-choice selection steps
A confirmation summary listing all collected values on the final step
do_not_place: Long scrollable content — split into additional steps instead

components.back: Button with variant='outline' — disabled on step 0
components.next: Button with default variant — label is 'Continue' on intermediate steps and the submit action label on the final step
components.feedback: toast() from sonner on successful submission

typical_content: Back button (outline, disabled on first step)

optional_dependencies.react-hook-form + @hookform/resolvers +: Add when steps have validation requirements beyond simple presence checks

constraints: Each step must contain exactly one logical decision or input group — split rather than combine unrelated fields
Back must always restore the previous step's field values — store all form state at the top level, not inside individual step components
Progress value must be derived from step index and total count — never hardcoded
The final step must show a read-only summary of all collected values before the submit action
Validation errors must appear inline below the offending field using text-sm text-destructive — never block advancement with an AlertDialog
Do not render sidebar, navigation, or unrelated controls — the only escape is an explicit Cancel link or 'Save and exit' Button (ghost variant)
The submit Button on the final step must call toast.success() on completion and reset or redirect as appropriate
agent_instructions: Identify the wizard's goal from the app description (e.g. project creation, onboarding, checkout, booking). Break that goal into 3–5 logical steps, each containing one coherent group of fields or a single choice. Generate appropriate Label + Input / Select / RadioGroup fields for each step. The final step must be a review screen that renders all collected values as a read-only summary. Wire back/next with a single top-level useState for step index and a single state object for form values. Use toast.success() on submission. If any step has required fields, add inline validation using text-sm text-destructive error messages before advancing.

layout_description: A full-width horizontal navigation bar pinned to the top of the viewport, with the main content area filling the remaining height below. The nav bar contains the app logo on the left, primary navigation links in the centre or left cluster, and utility controls (search, user avatar, theme toggle) on the right. Unlike the sidebar pattern, this layout gives the content area the full page width — preferred for apps where horizontal space matters, such as developer tools, docs platforms, and marketing-adjacent dashboards. Sub-navigation (tabs or a secondary bar) can appear below the top nav to scope content within a section.
html_structure:
import { NavigationMenu, NavigationMenuList, NavigationMenuItem, NavigationMenuLink } from '@/components/ui/navigation-menu' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent } from '@/components/ui/tabs' import { Breadcrumb, BreadcrumbList, BreadcrumbItem, BreadcrumbPage, BreadcrumbSeparator } from '@/components/ui/breadcrumb' export default function App() { return (
{/* Top nav */}
{/* Search, notifications, theme toggle, avatar */} U
{/* Optional sub-nav via Tabs */} {/*
Overview
*/} {/* Main content */}
{/* Optional breadcrumb */} {/* Page content */}
) }

components.search: Input with search icon, or CommandDialog triggered by Button with Kbd shortcut

topnav.block_alternative: site-header block (from @/components/blocks/site-header) — use when a pre-built responsive header with mobile hamburger is needed
typical_content: Avatar with DropdownMenu for account actions right-aligned

sub-nav.role: Secondary scoping within a section — optional

components.menubar: Menubar + MenubarMenu + MenubarTrigger + MenubarContent + MenubarItem — use for app-menu-style sub-navigation

typical_content: Section tabs: Overview, Settings, Members, Logs

components.content-body: Card + CardHeader + CardContent — primary grouping unit

typical_content: Content pattern appropriate to the section

constraints: NavigationMenu items must reflect real sections of the app — do not use placeholder names like 'Page 1'
The active nav item must be visually indicated — use a className or data-active approach
Keep the topnav to a single row — never wrap nav items to a second line
Max-width the content area with max-w-6xl mx-auto to prevent line lengths becoming too wide
On mobile, replace NavigationMenu with a Sheet-based drawer triggered by a hamburger Button
One default variant Button per visible content area
agent_instructions: Infer 3–5 primary navigation sections from the app description and populate NavigationMenuList with NavigationMenuLink items. Mark the first item active. If the app has sub-sections within a primary section, implement sub-navigation using Tabs. Add an Avatar with DropdownMenu for account actions. Populate the content area with the most relevant primary view. Include a Breadcrumb if the app has more than one level of navigation depth.

layout_description: A two-panel layout where a scrollable list or table on the left drives a detail view on the right. Selecting an item in the left panel updates the right panel without navigating away. The left panel is typically narrower (one-third or fixed width) and contains a searchable, filterable list of items. The right panel fills the remaining width and shows the full record for the selected item, including editable fields, related data, and actions. On mobile, the panels stack: the list is the default view, and selecting an item pushes the detail view full-screen.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { ResizablePanelGroup, ResizablePanel, ResizableHandle } from '@/components/ui/resizable' import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { cn } from '@/lib/utils' export default function App() { const [selected, setSelected] = useState(null) return (
{/* Optional top bar */}
{/* List panel */}
{/* List items — each a button/div with onClick */}
{/* Detail panel */} {selected ? (
{/* Detail content */}
) : (
{/* Empty — nothing selected */}
)}
) }

list-panel.role: Scrollable, searchable item list — drives selection

components.list-item: Plain div or button with cn() for selected state (bg-accent), containing item name, subtitle, and optional Badge
components.filter-tabs: Tabs + TabsList + TabsTrigger above the list if filtering by status is needed

typical_content: Search Input at the top of the panel
Scrollable list of items, each showing name + subtitle + status Badge

detail-panel.role: Full record view for the selected item

components.header: Avatar (for people) or icon + heading + Badge for status
components.fields: Card + CardContent with Label + value pairs, or editable Input / Select / Textarea fields
components.actions: Button group (default primary action + outline secondary actions)
components.tabs: Tabs + TabsList + TabsTrigger + TabsContent — use when the detail view has multiple sub-sections (Details, Activity, Related)
components.related-list: Table or plain list of related items
components.empty-state: Empty component centred in the panel when nothing is selected

typical_content: Record header: avatar or icon, name, status Badge
Action buttons: primary (Save / Send / Approve) + secondary (Edit / Delete)
Optional tabbed sub-sections for Activity, Notes, or Related items

constraints: Always wrap panels in ResizablePanelGroup — this gives the user control over the split ratio
The detail panel must render an Empty state when no item is selected — never show a blank white panel
Selected state must be stored at the top level (App.tsx), not inside the list component
ScrollArea must wrap both the list and the detail content independently — they scroll separately
On mobile (below md breakpoint), hide the ResizableHandle and stack panels: list visible by default, detail view on selection
List items must be keyboard-accessible — use button elements, not divs
agent_instructions: Generate 8–12 realistic sample items appropriate to the app domain (e.g. contacts, emails, issues, orders). Implement selected state at the top level. Render the list panel with a search Input that filters items by name client-side. Highlight the selected item with bg-accent. In the detail panel, show a full record view for the selected item including all relevant fields. Add an Empty component when nothing is selected. If the detail has natural sub-sections (e.g. Details + Activity), use Tabs. Add react-resizable-panels to dependencies.

layout_description: An infinite or large-area canvas surrounded by floating toolbars. Unlike other templates, the canvas is the primary content — it is not a region inside a layout, it IS the layout. A left toolbar holds drawing or editing tools. A top toolbar holds global actions and view controls. A right panel holds properties for the selected element. The canvas itself is a free-form space where content is positioned absolutely or rendered via a scene graph. This template is appropriate for diagram editors, whiteboard tools, visual builders, and node-based editors.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { Toggle, ToggleGroup, ToggleGroupItem } from '@/components/ui/toggle-group' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Tooltip, TooltipTrigger, TooltipContent, TooltipProvider } from '@/components/ui/tooltip' import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area' import { Slider } from '@/components/ui/slider' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label' export default function App() { const [zoom, setZoom] = useState(100) const [activeTool, setActiveTool] = useState('select') return (
{/* Top toolbar */}
{/* File actions: New, Open, Save */}
{/* Zoom control */} {zoom}% setZoom(v)} min={25} max={200} step={5} className="w-28" />
{/* Left tool palette */}
v && setActiveTool(v)} orientation="vertical"> Select
{/* Canvas */}
{/* Canvas content rendered here — absolutely positioned elements or SVG/canvas */}
{/* Right properties panel */}
Properties
{/* Selected element properties */}
) }

components.file-actions: Button (ghost, sm) for Save / Export / Undo / Redo
components.zoom: Slider for zoom level + span showing current percentage

do_not_place: Drawing tools (those go in the left toolbar)
Properties (those go in the right panel)

components.tools: ToggleGroup (type='single', orientation='vertical') + ToggleGroupItem for each tool
components.tooltips: Tooltip + TooltipTrigger + TooltipContent wrapping each ToggleGroupItem — always include, as tools are icon-only

do_not_place: Text labels on buttons — use Tooltip instead

components.surface: div with relative positioning, overflow-hidden, bg-muted/30 — canvas elements absolutely positioned inside
components.cursor: cursor-default for select tool, cursor-crosshair for drawing tools via cn()

typical_content: Absolutely positioned Card elements for node-based editors
Grid background pattern via Tailwind bg utilities
do_not_place: Fixed UI controls — those go in toolbars or the properties panel

properties-panel.role: Context-sensitive properties for the selected element

components.fields: Label + Input, Label + Select, Label + Slider for individual properties

typical_content: Style properties: fill colour, stroke, opacity
Empty state when nothing is selected: text-sm text-muted-foreground

optional_dependencies.@dnd-kit/core + @dnd-kit/sortable: Add when canvas elements need drag-to-reposition support

constraints: The outer container must be overflow-hidden — the canvas must not cause the page to scroll
Always wrap tool buttons in Tooltip so icon-only controls are accessible
The active tool must be reflected in canvas cursor style using cn()
ToggleGroup for tools must use type='single' so only one tool is active at a time
The properties panel must show a clear empty state when no element is selected
Zoom must be controlled via Slider with min=25, max=200, step=5 — display the value as an integer percentage
agent_instructions: Infer what kind of canvas app this is (diagram editor, whiteboard, node editor, visual builder) from the description. Implement a tool palette with at least a Select tool and 2–3 domain-appropriate drawing or editing tools using ToggleGroup. Wrap all tool buttons in Tooltip. Implement zoom state with a Slider. Render 3–5 sample elements on the canvas as positioned Cards or divs to demonstrate the layout is working. Populate the properties panel with fields relevant to the selected element type. The canvas cursor must change based on the active tool.

layout_description: A single scrollable column of homogeneous items — posts, events, notifications, or log entries — presented in reverse chronological order. A compose or create action is available at the top or via a prominent button. Filter controls let the user scope the feed by type, author, or date. Each item in the feed is a self-contained card with consistent structure: avatar or icon, author or source, timestamp, content body, and optional action row. The feed has a constrained max-width to maintain readable line lengths. A sidebar for navigation or supplementary info is optional.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { Card, CardContent } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs' import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' export default function App() { const [filter, setFilter] = useState('all') return (
{/* Top bar */}
{/* Compose area */}

components.input: Textarea for multi-line post content, or Input for single-line entries
components.submit: Button (default variant) — one per compose area

filter-bar.role: Scope the feed by category or relationship

components.tabs: Tabs + TabsList + TabsTrigger — preferred for 2–5 named filters
components.toggle-group: ToggleGroup + ToggleGroupItem — use for icon-based view toggles (list vs compact)

feed-items.role: The scrollable list of content items

components.item-card: Card + CardContent — one per feed item
components.avatar: Avatar + AvatarFallback (or AvatarImage) for author identity
components.status: Badge for item type or category
components.actions: Button (ghost, sm) for like, reply, share, or domain-equivalent actions
components.empty: Empty component when feed is empty or filter returns no results
components.loading: Skeleton matching the Card + Avatar layout

typical_content: Content body as p with text-sm
Action row: ghost Buttons for domain-appropriate interactions

constraints: Constrain the feed column to max-w-2xl mx-auto — never let items stretch full page width
Feed items must be in reverse chronological order (newest first)
Each feed item must have consistent structure: avatar + author + timestamp + content + actions
The compose area must appear above the filter bar, which appears above the feed
Filter tab changes must update the visible items client-side without a reload
Use realistic sample content — not 'Lorem ipsum post content'
agent_instructions: Infer the feed's content type from the app description (posts, activity events, log entries, notifications). Generate 6–8 realistic sample items with varied content and timestamps. Implement filter tabs that show relevant subsets. Build a compose area with Textarea and a submit Button that prepends a new item to the feed. Use Avatar + AvatarFallback with initials for each item author. Add domain-appropriate ghost Button actions per item (e.g. Like + Reply for social, Resolve + Assign for activity feeds).

layout_description: A full-width data table is the primary content of this layout. Above the table, a toolbar provides search, filter controls, and a primary action to create new records. Column headers are sortable. Rows are selectable, and a bulk action bar appears when rows are selected. Pagination sits below the table. The table can optionally support row expansion for inline detail, or a click-through that opens the master-detail or Sheet pattern. The outer shell can be the full page or nested inside a sidebar-content layout.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableHead, TableRow, TableCell } from '@/components/ui/table' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Checkbox } from '@/components/ui/checkbox' import { DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem } from '@/components/ui/dropdown-menu' import { Select, SelectTrigger, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select' import { Pagination, PaginationContent, PaginationItem, PaginationPrevious, PaginationNext, PaginationLink } from '@/components/ui/pagination' export default function App() { const [search, setSearch] = useState('') const [selected, setSelected] = useState([]) return (
{/* Toolbar */}
setSearch(e.target.value)} className="w-64" />
{/* Bulk action bar — visible when rows selected */} {selected.length > 0 && (
{selected.length} selected
)} {/* Table */}
{/* Column headers */} {/* Rows */} {/* Cells */}
{/* Pagination */} 1
) }

toolbar.role: Table-level controls: search, filter, and primary record creation action

components.search: Input with placeholder 'Search...' and controlled value
components.filter: Select + SelectTrigger + SelectContent + SelectItem for status/category filtering
components.secondary-actions: Button (outline) or DropdownMenu for export, import, bulk operations

toolbar.block_alternative: data-table block (from @/components/blocks/data-table) — provides a fully wired toolbar, column visibility toggle, and sorting out of the box via @tanstack/react-table. Use for production-quality tables with complex requirements.

components.sort-button: Button (ghost, size='sm') inside TableHead with sort icon and onClick handler
components.select-all: Checkbox in first TableHead column for row selection

typical_content: Actions column right-aligned (typically no header label)
do_not_place: Filter controls — those go in the toolbar above

components.status-cell: Badge inside TableCell for status values
components.row-checkbox: Checkbox in first TableCell for individual row selection
components.row-actions: DropdownMenu + DropdownMenuTrigger (three-dot icon Button) + DropdownMenuContent + DropdownMenuItem in last TableCell
components.empty: TableRow with single TableCell colSpan={columnCount} containing Empty component

typical_content: Data cells with appropriate typography (font-medium for primary identifier, text-muted-foreground for secondary)
Three-dot DropdownMenu for row actions (Edit, Delete, etc.)
do_not_place: Bulk controls — those go in the bulk action bar

components.pagination: Pagination + PaginationContent + PaginationItem + PaginationPrevious + PaginationNext + PaginationLink + PaginationEllipsis

do_not_place: Row count info — place that as plain text next to the Pagination component

optional_dependencies.@tanstack/react-table: Add when using the data-table block, or when column sorting/filtering/visibility needs to be production-quality

constraints: Wrap the Table in a border rounded-lg overflow-hidden div so the table has visible containment
Always include a row checkbox column and a bulk action bar that appears when rows are selected
Status values must use Badge, not plain text
Row actions must use a DropdownMenu — never expose Delete as a bare button in the row
The empty state (no rows matching search/filter) must render inside the TableBody, not replace the table
Client-side filtering: implement search and status filter as controlled state that filters the sample data array
agent_instructions: Identify the data entity from the app description (users, orders, tasks, products, etc.). Define 5–7 relevant columns and generate 10–15 realistic sample rows. Implement client-side search filtering on the primary identifier column. Implement a status filter Select with domain-appropriate values. Add row-level DropdownMenu actions (at minimum Edit and Delete). Implement checkbox selection with a bulk action bar. Wrap in Pagination (client-side, 10 rows per page). Use the data-table block if the app description implies complex table requirements like column visibility or advanced sorting.

layout_description: A two-pane layout with an editor or input area on the left and a live rendered preview on the right. The two panes are separated by a draggable ResizableHandle. The left pane is for authoring — text, code, form fields, or a structured editor. The right pane shows the rendered or processed output in real time as the user types or makes changes. A toolbar at the top provides file-level actions (save, export, format) and view controls (toggle preview, swap panes). Used for Markdown editors, email builders, code playgrounds, template editors, and configuration tools.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { ResizablePanelGroup, ResizablePanel, ResizableHandle } from '@/components/ui/resizable' import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger, TabsContent } from '@/components/ui/tabs' import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' export default function App() { const [source, setSource] = useState('') return (
{/* Toolbar */}
{/* Split panes */} {/* Editor pane */}
Editor

components.file-actions: Button (ghost, sm) for Save / Export / Format / Clear
components.status: Badge (secondary) for file name, word count, or save status
components.view-toggle: Tabs or ToggleGroup to switch between Edit-only / Split / Preview-only modes

editor-pane.role: Authoring surface — where the user creates or edits content

components.simple-editor: Textarea with font-mono text-sm, resize-none, border-0, full height
components.rich-editor: Multiple Input / Textarea / Select fields in a form layout — use when the input is structured (template fields, config, form builder)
components.pane-label: span with text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide in a border-b header row

typical_content: Textarea for Markdown, code, or plain text authoring
Structured form fields for template or config editing

preview-pane.role: Live rendered output derived from the editor state

components.rendered-output: div with prose styling for Markdown, or live component render for template preview
components.pane-label: span with text-xs font-medium text-muted-foreground uppercase tracking-wide in a border-b header row
components.empty: Centered text-muted-foreground message when source is empty

typical_content: Live preview of a template or email layout
Formatted output of code or config

constraints: The preview must update reactively as the source state changes — no manual refresh button
Textarea in the editor pane must have resize-none and border-0 with focus-visible:ring-0 to feel embedded
Both panes must scroll independently via their own overflow or ScrollArea
The outer container must be overflow-hidden with flex-1 on the ResizablePanelGroup to fill the viewport
For Markdown preview, use dangerouslySetInnerHTML only with sanitised content — prefer a simple custom renderer for sample purposes
agent_instructions: Identify the editor type from the app description (Markdown, code, email template, config, etc.). Pre-populate the source Textarea with a realistic example relevant to the domain. Implement the preview pane to render the source content live: for Markdown use a simple custom renderer or dangerouslySetInnerHTML with basic HTML transformation; for code use a pre + code block; for templates render the interpolated output. Add Save and Export (copy to clipboard) buttons in the toolbar. Add react-resizable-panels to dependencies.

layout_description: A scrollable message thread with a pinned composer at the bottom. Messages alternate between user and assistant (or participant) sides, with clear visual distinction between sender roles. A session list sidebar on the left enables multi-conversation apps — clicking a session loads that thread. The composer supports multi-line input and a send action. The message list auto-scrolls to the latest message on new input. This template covers AI chat interfaces, support chat tools, team messaging channels, and any turn-based conversation UI.

session-list.role: Multi-conversation navigation — optional for single-conversation apps

components.new-button: Button (outline, full-width) for starting a new conversation
components.session-items: div or button per session with title, timestamp snippet, and optional unread Badge
components.search: Input above the list for filtering sessions by title

session-list.taiso_alternative: taiso-chat service component (from @/components/taiso/taiso-chat) — use when the chat needs to connect to the Taiso LLM API with built-in session management, streaming, and choice selectors
typical_content: Session list items with title and relative timestamp

components.scroll: ScrollArea with flex-1 — auto-scrolls to bottom on new message via useRef + scrollIntoView
components.message-bubble: div with cn() toggling bg-primary text-primary-foreground (user) vs bg-muted (assistant/other)
components.loading: Spinner or animated Skeleton for streaming/pending assistant response

components.send-button: Button (default) — disabled when input is empty
components.attachments: Button (ghost, icon-only) for file attachment if needed

typical_content: Textarea with 2–3 rows, grows with content

constraints: The message list must auto-scroll to the bottom when a new message is added — use useRef + scrollIntoView
Enter key in Textarea must send the message; Shift+Enter must insert a newline
The Send Button must be disabled when the input is empty or whitespace-only
User messages must be visually distinct from assistant/other messages — use bg-primary vs bg-muted
Constrain the message list and composer to max-w-2xl mx-auto to maintain readable line lengths
Pre-populate the thread with 3–5 realistic sample messages so the layout is immediately visible
agent_instructions: Infer the conversation type from the app description (AI assistant, support chat, team messaging). Pre-populate messages with 4–6 realistic sample exchanges. Implement send functionality: user messages append immediately, and for AI chat apps add a simulated assistant response with a short setTimeout. Wire Enter-to-send. If the app is AI-focused, note in a comment that the send handler can be wired to the Taiso LLM API or the taiso-chat service component. If multi-session is relevant, populate the session list with 3–4 realistic session titles.

layout_description: A two-column settings page with a section navigation list on the left and a form-heavy detail pane on the right. The left column lists all settings categories (Account, Security, Notifications, Billing, etc.) as clickable items. Selecting a category scrolls or routes to that section's form in the right pane. Each section contains a heading, a brief description, and a group of labelled form fields. A save action is available per section or as a sticky footer bar. On mobile, the section list collapses to a Select or Sheet-based navigation.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Switch } from '@/components/ui/switch' import { Select, SelectTrigger, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { cn } from '@/lib/utils' import { toast } from 'sonner' const SECTIONS = [ { id: 'account', label: 'Account' }, { id: 'security', label: 'Security' }, { id: 'notifications', label: 'Notifications' }, ] export default function App() { const [active, setActive] = useState('account') return (
Settings
{/* Section nav */}
{SECTIONS.map(s => ( ))}
{/* Form pane */}
{active === 'account' && ( Account Update your account information. {/* Fields */}
)}
) }

components.nav-items: button elements with cn() toggling bg-accent (active) vs text-muted-foreground hover:bg-accent/50
components.mobile-fallback: Select + SelectTrigger + SelectContent + SelectItem — use below md breakpoint

form-groups.role: The settings form for the active section

components.text-field: Label + Input in a flex flex-col gap-1.5 wrapper
components.toggle-field: Label + Switch in a flex items-center justify-between row
components.danger-section: Card with border-destructive for irreversible actions (delete account, etc.)
components.avatar-upload: Avatar + AvatarFallback with a Button (outline) for changing profile picture

typical_content: Security: current password, new password, 2FA Switch
Billing: current plan Badge, upgrade Button
do_not_place: Unrelated sections — render one section's Card(s) at a time

save-bar.role: Persist changes for the current section

components.save: Button (default) — 'Save changes', calls toast.success() on click
components.cancel: Button (outline or ghost) — 'Cancel', resets form state

typical_content: Save changes Button right-aligned inside CardContent
Cancel Button left of Save for destructive-risk sections

constraints: Render only the active section's Card(s) in the form pane — do not render all sections simultaneously
Every form field must have a Label with a matching htmlFor / id pair
Save must call toast.success() — never navigate away or reload
Dangerous / irreversible actions (delete account, revoke access) must be in a separate Card with border-destructive and a confirmation AlertDialog
Section nav items must have clear active state using bg-accent
Do not use Form + FormField (react-hook-form) unless the description specifically requires field validation — Label + Input is sufficient for most settings forms
agent_instructions: Infer 3–5 relevant settings sections from the app description. For each section, generate appropriate Label + Input / Switch / Select fields. Implement active section state and render only the active section's content. Add toast.success() on each Save button. If the app has user accounts, include an Account section with name, email, and avatar. If the app has notifications, implement a Notifications section with Switch toggles per notification type. Add a Danger Zone card in the last section with a delete/reset action behind an AlertDialog confirmation.

layout_description: A minimal, centered card on a full-viewport background, containing an authentication form. The background uses the app's primary or muted background color. The card holds the app logo or name, the form fields, and a primary submit action. Secondary actions (sign up / log in toggle, forgot password link) appear below the submit button. Social auth buttons (Sign in with Google, etc.) appear above or below the form with a visual separator. Multiple screen variants share the same centered-card layout: Login, Sign Up, Forgot Password, and Email Verification.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardFooter } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { toast } from 'sonner' export default function App() { const [screen, setScreen] = useState('login') const [email, setEmail] = useState('') const [password, setPassword] = useState('') return (
{screen === 'login' ? 'Sign in' : screen === 'signup' ? 'Create account' : 'Reset password'} {screen === 'login' ? 'Enter your credentials to continue' : screen === 'signup' ? 'Fill in your details to get started' : "We'll send you a reset link"} {/* Social auth */}
or
{/* Form fields */} {screen === 'signup' && (
)}
setEmail(e.target.value)} placeholder="you@example.com" />
{screen !== 'forgot' && (
{screen === 'login' && ( )}
setPassword(e.target.value)} />
)}

{screen === 'login' ? "Don't have an account? " : 'Already have an account? '}

) }

logo.role: App identity at the top of the auth card

components.descriptor: CardDescription with a short screen-specific message

typical_content: Short action prompt as CardDescription: 'Sign in to continue', 'Create your account'

components.social-auth: Button (outline, full-width) for OAuth providers, above a Separator with 'or' label
components.fields: Label + Input (email type for email, password type for password) in flex flex-col gap-1.5 wrappers
components.forgot-link: button element with text-xs text-muted-foreground hover:underline — right-aligned next to the Password Label

form-card.block_alternative: login-form block (from @/components/blocks/login-form) — use this when a fully pre-built, styled login form is sufficient without customisation
typical_content: Password Input with Forgot password link
Toggle link to sign up / sign in in CardFooter

components.toggle: Plain button element with underline + text-foreground — 'Sign up' / 'Sign in' toggle
components.terms: text-xs text-muted-foreground with inline links for Terms and Privacy if required

constraints: Always use bg-muted/40 on the outer wrapper so the card has visible lift from the background
Input type must be correct: type='email' for email, type='password' for password
The submit Button must be full-width (w-full)
Never navigate on submit in the prototype — use toast.success() as a stand-in
Implement screen switching (login / signup / forgot) as local state so all variants are demoable
The login-form block is a fast alternative — reference it if the app description doesn't require customisation
agent_instructions: Implement all three auth screen variants (login, signup, forgot password) as conditional renders controlled by a screen state variable. Wire the toggle links and forgot-password link to switch between screens. Add a social auth Button with an outline variant above the form separator. Call toast.success() on form submit. If the app description suggests a specific brand, update the CardTitle. Reference the login-form block as an alternative if minimal customisation is needed.

layout_description: A three-column layout — section navigation on the far left, an item list in the centre, and an action panel on the right — designed for managing a set of actionable items. Unlike a pure feed, inbox items carry read/unread state, triage actions (resolve, assign, archive, snooze), and often belong to categories (Todo, In Progress, Done). The centre column is the primary focus: items are sorted by recency or priority, with visual weight on unread items. The right panel shows the selected item's detail and available actions. Used for notification centres, support queues, task inboxes, and review queues.

filter-tabs.role: Section navigation: category or triage-state filters

components.nav-items: button elements with cn() for active state, each showing label + unread count Badge
components.count-badge: Badge (secondary, small) showing unread count per section

item-list.role: Scrollable list of inbox items for the active section

components.item-row: div/button with border-b, selected state (bg-accent), unread state (font-medium), Avatar + title + preview + timestamp
components.unread-dot: div with w-2 h-2 rounded-full bg-primary for unread indicator
components.empty: Empty component when section has no items

do_not_place: Triage action buttons — those go in the action panel

action-panel.role: Full item view with triage actions

components.header: Item title as heading + sender Avatar + timestamp
components.body: ScrollArea wrapping the item's full content
components.triage-actions: ButtonGroup or flex row of Button (outline/ghost) — Resolve, Archive, Snooze, Assign
components.reply: Textarea + Button for reply/response if applicable
components.empty: Centred text-sm text-muted-foreground when nothing is selected

typical_content: Triage action buttons: Resolve, Snooze, Archive
Reply composer if the inbox type supports replies

constraints: Unread items must be visually heavier than read items — use font-medium and an unread dot
The action panel must show an empty state when nothing is selected
Section nav must show unread counts as Badge next to each label
Triage actions must update item state immediately (optimistic UI) — mark as read, move to Done, etc.
Item list and action panel scroll independently via their own ScrollArea instances
agent_instructions: Infer the inbox type from the app description (notifications, support tickets, task queue, review queue, etc.). Generate 6–10 sample items with realistic titles, senders, previews, and timestamps — mix read and unread states. Implement section filtering (Inbox / Done / Snoozed) as client-side state. Wire item selection to show detail in the action panel. Implement at least Resolve and Archive triage actions that move items between sections. Add a reply composer in the action panel if the inbox type supports responses.

layout_description: A modal or overlay layer that sits above the app shell. This template is not a standalone page layout — it is a component pattern used within any other template. It covers three distinct variants: a Dialog for confirmation and data-entry modals, a Sheet for side panels and detail drawers, and a Command palette (⌘K) for keyboard-driven navigation and action search. Each variant has distinct sizing and interaction models but shares the same underlying scrim-and-panel structure.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { Dialog, DialogTrigger, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogFooter, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogClose } from '@/components/ui/dialog' import { AlertDialog, AlertDialogTrigger, AlertDialogContent, AlertDialogHeader, AlertDialogFooter, AlertDialogTitle, AlertDialogDescription, AlertDialogAction, AlertDialogCancel } from '@/components/ui/alert-dialog' import { Sheet, SheetTrigger, SheetContent, SheetHeader, SheetFooter, SheetTitle, SheetDescription, SheetClose } from '@/components/ui/sheet' import { Command, CommandInput, CommandList, CommandEmpty, CommandGroup, CommandItem, CommandSeparator, CommandShortcut } from '@/components/ui/command' import { Popover, PopoverTrigger, PopoverContent } from '@/components/ui/popover' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label' import { Kbd } from '@/components/ui/kbd' import { toast } from 'sonner' // Variant 1: Confirmation dialog Are you sure? This action cannot be undone. Cancel toast.success('Deleted')}>Delete // Variant 2: Data-entry dialog Create item Fill in the details below.
// Variant 3: Command palette (⌘K) // Wrap in Popover or Dialog open state triggered by Cmd+K listener No results found. New project⌘N Open settings⌘, Go to dashboard

scrim.role: Background overlay that dims the app shell

components.command-wrapper: Dialog with full-screen content area for command palette — or Popover for inline

typical_content: Handled automatically by Dialog, AlertDialog, and Sheet components
do_not_place: App content — scrim is managed by the component, not authored manually

modal-card.role: The floating panel — type determines size and position

components.confirmation: AlertDialog + AlertDialogContent + AlertDialogHeader + AlertDialogFooter — use for irreversible actions only
components.data-entry: Dialog + DialogContent + DialogHeader + DialogFooter — use for forms and create/edit flows
components.side-panel: Sheet + SheetContent (side='right') + SheetHeader + SheetFooter — use for detail views and extended forms
components.command-palette: Command + CommandInput + CommandList + CommandGroup + CommandItem + CommandShortcut — use for keyboard-driven search and navigation (requires cmdk dependency)

body.role: Form fields or content inside the modal

components.fields: Label + Input / Textarea / Select in flex flex-col gap-1.5 wrappers
components.form: Form + FormField + FormControl + FormMessage when validation is needed

components.dialog-footer: DialogFooter with Cancel (outline via DialogClose) + primary action Button
components.sheet-footer: SheetFooter with SheetClose + primary action Button

components.input: CommandInput — built into Command, handles filtering automatically

constraints: Use AlertDialog for irreversible/destructive confirmations — never use Dialog for these
Use Dialog for data-entry and create/edit flows
Use Sheet for detail side-panels that need more space than a Dialog
DialogFooter Cancel must use DialogClose asChild to close the dialog on click
Command palette must respond to Cmd+K — wire a useEffect keyboard listener
Never put a Delete action directly in a Dialog's primary Button — always use AlertDialog for destructive actions
agent_instructions: Identify which modal variant(s) the app needs from the description. For most apps, implement at minimum: a Dialog for the primary create/edit action, and an AlertDialog for any delete/destructive action. If the app benefits from keyboard navigation (developer tools, productivity apps), add a Command palette triggered by Cmd+K. Populate the Command palette with 6–10 relevant actions and navigation items grouped by category. Wire all modals to toast.success() on confirm. Add cmdk to dependencies only if the Command palette is implemented.

layout_description: A two-pane reading and writing layout. The left pane shows reference content — source documents, search results, context, or retrieved citations. The right pane shows generated or processed output — an answer, a draft, a filled template, or a transformed result. A divider between the panes is draggable. An optional citation rail on the far right links numbered references in the output back to their source passages. A top toolbar holds the query input or task description, and controls for refreshing or re-running the generation. Used for AI research tools, document processors, and reference-driven writing tools.
html_structure:
import { useState } from 'react' import { ResizablePanelGroup, ResizablePanel, ResizableHandle } from '@/components/ui/resizable' import { ScrollArea } from '@/components/ui/scroll-area' import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input' import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Spinner } from '@/components/ui/spinner' import { cn } from '@/lib/utils' export default function App() { const [query, setQuery] = useState('') const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false) return (
{/* Toolbar: query input */}
setQuery(e.target.value)} placeholder="Ask a question or describe your task..." className="flex-1" onKeyDown={e => { if (e.key === 'Enter') { /* trigger run */ } }} />
{/* Source pane */}
Sources
{/* Source cards */}

{/* Result pane */}
Result
{/* Generated result */}
) }

components.run-button: Button — shows Spinner when loading, disabled when empty
components.status: Badge (secondary) for result status or source count

source-pane.role: Reference content that informs the result

components.source-card: Card + CardHeader (CardTitle for source title) + CardContent (excerpt text)
components.citation-number: Badge (outline, small) as superscript reference number
components.empty: Empty component or text-sm text-muted-foreground when no sources loaded

source-pane.taiso_alternative: search-bar service component (from @/components/taiso/search-bar) — use when the source pane needs hybrid vector+text search against a Taiso knowledge base
typical_content: Source cards with title, URL/reference, and excerpt
Citation number Badge on each source

components.content: div with prose-appropriate text styling for rendered output
components.copy-button: Button (ghost, sm) in the pane header for copying result to clipboard
components.loading: Spinner centred in the pane while generation is in progress
components.empty: Text-sm text-muted-foreground prompt to run a query

typical_content: Numbered inline citations linking to source cards

citation-rail.role: Numbered reference index linking result citations to sources — optional

components.citation-links: Button (ghost, size='sm') per citation number that scrolls the source pane to that source

typical_content: Clicking scrolls source pane to the matching card

constraints: The Run Button must be disabled when the query Input is empty
Show Spinner in the Run Button while loading — never disable the UI entirely
Source and result panes scroll independently via separate ScrollArea instances
Pre-populate the source pane with 3–4 realistic sample source cards so the layout is immediately visible
The result pane must show a helpful empty state prompt when no query has been run yet
agent_instructions: Identify the app's domain (research assistant, document analyser, code explainer, etc.). Pre-populate 3–4 realistic source cards in the source pane. Pre-populate the result pane with a sample answer that references the sources. Implement the Run Button with a simulated loading state (useState + setTimeout). Wire Enter-to-run on the query Input. Add a Copy button that writes the result text to the clipboard via navigator.clipboard. If the app description implies connecting to the Taiso search API, add a comment indicating the search-bar service component can be used for the source pane. Add react-resizable-panels to dependencies.

layout_description: A vertically scrolling public marketing page with a sticky top navigation bar, a hero section, one or more feature or benefit sections, social proof (testimonials or logos), a pricing or comparison section, and a footer. The page is primarily read-only — CTAs direct the user to sign up or start a trial. The layout is full-width with max-width constrained content columns. This template is used for SaaS product homepages, product launch pages, and campaign landing pages. It is the only template with no persistent app chrome — the nav bar is the only UI element present across all sections.
html_structure:
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button' import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge' import { Card, CardContent, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardFooter } from '@/components/ui/card' import { Separator } from '@/components/ui/separator' import { Avatar, AvatarFallback } from '@/components/ui/avatar' export default function App() { return (
{/* Sticky nav */}
Features Pricing
{/* Hero */}

{/* Features */}

{/* Pricing */}

Pricing

{/* Pricing cards */}
{/* Footer */}
Privacy Terms
) }

components.links: Plain anchor tags with text-sm text-muted-foreground hover:text-foreground
components.cta: Button (ghost, sm) for Sign in + Button (sm) for primary CTA

typical_content: 3–4 anchor links to page sections (Features, Pricing, About) — hidden on mobile

hero.role: Above-the-fold section — primary value proposition and CTAs

components.eyebrow: Badge (secondary) for a short tagline or launch announcement
components.headline: h1 with text-4xl font-bold font-heading tracking-tight
components.ctas: Two Buttons (size='lg'): default primary CTA + outline secondary CTA

typical_content: Headline: clear value proposition in 8 words or fewer
Sub-headline: 1–2 sentences expanding on the benefit

components.section-heading: h2 with text-2xl font-bold font-heading text-center
components.feature-cards: Card + CardHeader (CardTitle + CardDescription) in a 3-column grid

typical_content: 3–6 feature Cards, each with a short title and 1–2 sentence description

social-proof.role: Testimonials, customer logos, or usage stats — optional

components.testimonials: Card + CardContent with blockquote + Avatar + AvatarFallback + author name
components.stats: Three-column flex row with large number + muted label

components.featured-plan: Card with border-2 border-primary and a Badge ('Most popular') in CardHeader
components.price: Large text with font-bold + small text-muted-foreground for /month
components.features-list: ul with li items, checkmark prefix using text-primary
components.cta: Button in CardFooter — default for featured plan, outline for others

components.links: anchor tags for Privacy, Terms, Contact

constraints: The nav must be sticky (sticky top-0 z-50) with bg-background/80 so it remains readable while scrolling
Constrain all section content to max-w-5xl mx-auto (or max-w-3xl for text-heavy hero/cta sections)
Use Separator between major sections rather than large padding alone
The featured pricing plan must use border-2 border-primary — not a different background color
Never use hardcoded colors — use text-muted-foreground, text-foreground, bg-background, etc.
Anchor links in the nav must use href='#section-id' matching the section's id attribute
agent_instructions: Infer the product's value proposition, target user, and 3–6 key features from the app description. Write a concise headline (≤8 words), sub-headline (1–2 sentences), and 3 feature cards with realistic names and descriptions. Generate 3 pricing tiers appropriate to the product (Free/Pro/Enterprise or similar) with a feature checklist for each. Mark the middle tier as featured. Add 2 testimonials with realistic names and quotes. Use realistic stats in the social proof section. Ensure all section anchor links work.

layout_description: A full-page or full-region state that appears when there is nothing to show or when something has gone wrong. The layout is a centred vertical stack: an illustration or icon at the top, a headline, a short explanatory message, and a single recovery action. This template covers four variants: Empty (a list or collection has no items yet), First-use (the user hasn't created anything yet — encourage them to start), Error (something went wrong — offer retry or support), and 404 (the page doesn't exist — offer navigation home). All variants share the same centred layout. This is used both as a full-page template and as an inline region inside another template.

illustration.role: Visual indicator of the state type

components.icon-box: div with w-16 h-16 rounded-2xl bg-muted flex items-center justify-center wrapping a Lucide icon
components.empty-component: Empty (from @/components/ui/empty) — handles icon, title, description, and action as props; use this for inline empty states within a larger layout

typical_content: For empty state: inbox, folder, or domain-specific icon
For error: alert-circle or x-circle icon
For first-use: sparkles or plus-circle icon

headline.role: Short, specific description of the state

components.title: h2 with text-xl font-bold font-heading text-center

typical_content: Empty: 'No tasks yet', 'Your inbox is empty', 'Nothing here'
do_not_place: Long sentences — the headline is 3–5 words max

typical_content: Empty: 'Get started by creating your first item.'
Error: 'Please try again, or contact support if the problem persists.'
404: 'The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.'

components.secondary: Button (ghost, sm) — optional escape hatch (Go home, Contact support)

do_not_place: Multiple primary actions — one recovery action only

constraints: Use the Empty component from @/components/ui/empty for inline empty states within tables, lists, and panels — it handles the icon/title/description/action layout automatically
Use the full-page custom layout only for full-viewport states (404, full-page error)
One primary action only — do not show multiple default-variant Buttons
The headline must be specific to the state: 'No tasks yet' is better than 'Empty'
Never show a technical error message or stack trace in the message — keep it human-readable
Constrain the content to max-w-sm and centre it — do not stretch the empty state full-width
agent_instructions: Identify which empty/error state variant is needed from the app description (empty list, first-use, error, 404). For inline empty states within tables or lists, use the Empty component with title, description, and action props. For full-page states, implement the centred stack layout with an appropriate Lucide icon in a rounded bg-muted box, a specific headline, a one-sentence message, and a single primary Button. If building a 404 page, add a secondary 'Go home' ghost Button. Choose an icon that matches the content type being empty (e.g. inbox icon for empty inbox, file-question for 404).
